title: Burgers bore in u colliding with a bright NLS soliton in N
description: Initialize u as a smoothed Burgers bore (descending step), N as a bright NLS soliton placed to the bore's left moving rightward (via initial phase gradient), and phi consistent. Watch the interaction: does the soliton transmit (refract through bore), reflect, get captured, or destroyed? Direct analog of BKdV T_C, now with NLS-Madelung dispersion replacing KdV linear dispersion.
ic: kappa = 1 (focusing). u(x,0) = 1.0 * (1 - tanh(x/0.5))/2 (bore: u_L=1.0, u_R=0, width 0.5 centered at 0). N(x,0) = 1.0 * sech^2(x + 8) (bright soliton, amplitude 1.0, at x=-8). phi(x,0) = 0.6*x (so phi_x = 0.6, the soliton moves right at speed ~0.6). [m(x,0) = u - N*phi_x is NOT zero — generic off-M_cs IC.]
output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256). Save 5+ snapshots so the bore-soliton encounter (~t=8 to 14) is visible.
phenomenon_target: Final N should still contain a recognizable peak with amplitude >= 0.3 (soliton survives some interaction). u should stay bounded (|u_max| < 5). Bore should not have blown up. Bonus: compute ||m||_2(t) and observe whether it decreases through the interaction (compound-soliton attractor signature).
